description_long: This object is a flintlock blunderbuss pistol, characterized by its short, wide, flared barrel and wooden stock. The barrel is metallic and widens dramatically at the muzzle, a hallmark of blunderbuss firearms designed for close-range use. The stock and grip are made of wood, smoothly contoured for ergonomic handling, with a dark finish at the butt and grip ends. The firing mechanism is a classic flintlock, with a visible hammer and trigger guard made of metal. The overall design is compact, with a single-shot loading system and no visible magazine. The object has a historical appearance, reminiscent of firearms used in the 17th to 18th centuries, and would be a rare sight in modern urban environments.
description: A compact flintlock blunderbuss pistol with a wide, flared metal barrel and a smoothly contoured wooden stock, featuring a classic hammer and trigger mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the blunderbuss, highlighting the flared barrel, wooden stock, and flintlock mechanism.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the wide, trumpet-shaped muzzle and the alignment of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden grip, metal barrel, and the flintlock firing mechanism from the other side.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the length of the barrel and the curvature of the wooden stock.
